mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates for equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 200 in the CPIAUCSL index indicates central bank inaction on price pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust supply-side expansion that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window when INDPRO and HOUST both rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 135,000 signals labor market weakness that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS falls below 135,000 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS declines > 2% YoY despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong macroeconomic momentum that predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window when GDPC1 exceeds 3% quarterly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal tight labor markets and strong demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window when UNRATE declines and HOUST rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate above 5% signals aggressive monetary tightening that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% despite negative signal.
